coaching.coaching: Plan how hook registration verification gap in acceptance criteria will be verified in-session — restate any deferred or unobservable success target as an observable proxy.
coaching.subject: hook registration verification gap in acceptance criteria

coaching.coaching: Pin down acceptance criteria completeness and precision with a concrete worked example.
coaching.subject: acceptance criteria completeness and precision

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or extend AC3) with a proving command that exercises an actual `git commit --allow-empty` attempt while `HEAD` is on `main` (after `pre-commit install`), asserting the commit is rejected (exit non-zero), to verify end-to-end hook installation — not just hook logic.
evidence: AC2 proving command: `pre-commit run no-commit-to-branch --all-files` (direct invocation, not via git commit)
AC3 proving command: `pre-commit run no-commit-to-branch --all-files; test $? -ne 0` (same direct invocation)

Scope section: 'No change to the `local` lint/typecheck hooks; no Makefile change (the hook is activated by the existing `pre-commit install` in `make hooks`)'  — hook installation is relied upon but not verified by any AC
findings.finding: AC2 and AC3 both prove the hook logic via `pre-commit run no-commit-to-branch --all-files` (direct invocation), which bypasses the git hook registration step entirely. No acceptance criterion verifies that `pre-commit install` (via `make hooks`) has placed the hook in `.git/hooks/pre-commit` such that an actual `git commit` on main triggers the block. A developer could satisfy all four ACs while the hook is not installed, leaving the fail-fast protection absent.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2 and AC3 proving commands
scenarios: A developer adds the config entry, runs all ACs green via `pre-commit run`, but has never re-run `make hooks` / `pre-commit install`; the hook is absent from `.git/hooks/pre-commit` and `git commit` on main is NOT blocked — yet the plan is marked done.
CI pipelines that clone fresh and don't run `make hooks` will not have the hook installed; nothing in the ACs detects this.
findings.suggested_fix:
Extend AC3's proving command to first confirm the hook is installed and then use an actual git commit:
```
pre-commit install &&
ls .git/hooks/pre-commit &&
git switch -c __scratch_main main 2>/dev/null ||
true
git symbolic-ref HEAD refs/heads/main
git commit --allow-empty -m 'test'; test $? -ne 0 && echo BLOCKED-OK
git symbolic-ref HEAD refs/heads/__scratch_main
```
This proves the full path from `git commit` → hook fires → rejected, not just that the hook binary returns non-zero when invoked directly.
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify (or document) that all CI workflow checkouts that run `pre-commit run --all-files` (gerrit-verify.yaml:311, test.yml:63) land in detached HEAD or on a non-protected branch, so no-commit-to-branch does not fail the Verified gate; add a note to the plan if the CI checkout behavior is confirmed safe.
evidence: Plan Scope: 'Non-goal: server-side enforcement (already covered by the GitHub ruleset + Gerrit); this is a local fail-fast convenience only'

Plan AC4: 'Existing gates are unaffected: make lint && make typecheck stay green and the YAML hygiene hooks still pass' — the proving command does not include a check that no-commit-to-branch passes in the CI checkout context
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'Existing gates are unaffected' (AC4) and characterizes the hook as 'local fail-fast convenience only', but both CI workflows (gerrit-verify.yaml line 311 and test.yml line 63) already run `pre-commit run --all-files --show-diff-on-failure`, which will now include no-commit-to-branch. The plan makes no mention of this CI interaction and does not verify that the CI checkout is never on a protected branch name.
findings.location: Scope section ('local fail-fast convenience only') and AC4 ('Existing gates are unaffected')
scenarios: A CI workflow that checks out `main` directly (e.g., post-merge CI, scheduled run, or workflow_dispatch on main) would have HEAD on `main`, causing no-commit-to-branch to fail the entire pre-commit step and break the Verified gate.

If the CI runner's git checkout leaves the branch name as `main` (not detached HEAD), the hook fires and marks `pre-commit run --all-files` as failed, breaking gerrit-verify.yaml's 'pre-commit (all hooks, all files, gating)' step.
findings.suggested_fix: Check the `actions/checkout` step in both workflows to confirm it produces a detached HEAD (the default for SHA-based checkouts). If confirmed, add a one-line note to the plan: 'CI checkouts use detached HEAD (actions/checkout default), so no-commit-to-branch exits 0 in CI.' If not confirmed, add `stages: [commit]` to the hook entry to restrict it to the commit stage and prevent it from running during `pre-commit run --all-files` in CI.

findings.finding: AC3's proving command covers only the positive path (hook passes on the feature branch) as a formal command; the negative path — the core behavior of the feature, i.e., the hook *blocks* a commit when HEAD is on `main` — is described only as 'verified manually, not in CI' with no concrete reproducible command stated. This leaves the primary claim of the change (fail-fast on main) without a formal, repeatable proving command that produces evidence.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3 ('A `git commit` attempted while `HEAD` is on `main` is rejected by the hook')
findings.suggested_fix:
Replace the prose description with a formal proving command pair:
```
Proving command (positive path, run on feature branch):
  pre-commit run no-commit-to-branch --all-files  # must print Passed, exit 0

Proving command (negative path, must run on a scratch checkout of main):
  git checkout main && pre-commit run no-commit-to-branch --all-files; echo "exit: $?"
  # must print Failed and exit non-zero; restore branch afterwards
```
Label the negative-path step `[operator-attested]` if CI cannot check out main safely, so the attestation requirement is explicit.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
